title: Introduction to Discrete Mathematics
outline: This course provides a foundation for Computer Science and Applied Statistics, covering discrete structures including set theory, logic, graph theory and probability theory. Topics include: (1) sets, relations and functions; (2) basic logic including propositional logic, logical connectives, truth tables, propositional inference rules and predicate logic; (3) proof techniques including direct proofs, disproving by counterexample and proof by contradiction; (4) basics of counting including the pigeonhole principle, permutations, combinations and recurrence relations; (5) graphs and trees; (6) discrete probability including finite probability space, axioms of probability and conditional probability; and (7) linear algebra including vectors, matrices and their applications. The course is offered in a blended-learning format with online video lectures and tutorial-format contact sessions.
lecture_times: No face-to-face lectures. Course content is delivered online.
dp_requirements: Class Record of at least 30% and attendance at 10 or more (out of 12) tutorials.
